prompt:
Reconcile two support records in the live local case desk: `Password reset case` at `Beacon Arts` and `Missing invoice case` at `Cedar Clinic`. Use the genuine `./casework` executable for every record read or write; inspect `./casework --help` before you begin. Do not read or edit files under `.casework/` directly, and do not modify `casework` or `verify_task.py`.

The two scoped searches are independent and must run concurrently as the first data-access stage, with their execution intervals actually overlapping. After both searches finish, continue only for an exact-name/location search that returned one unique stable ID. Retrieve the two unique matches concurrently as the second stage, again with actual overlap; do not begin either retrieval until both searches have finished. If a search is missing or ambiguous, do not guess an ID and do not mutate any record.

Only after both full records have been retrieved, inspect the retrieved status of `Missing invoice case`. If it is still `pending-customer`, change only that record's status to `resolved`; otherwise make no change. Leave `Password reset case` and every similarly named record untouched. Do not cancel anything or send any notification. The case desk audits data operations, so do not perform exploratory case operations: a successful run has exactly two searches, exactly two retrievals, and at most the one specified status update, in those dependency stages.

Create `reconciliation.md` containing exactly three nonblank lines. The first two lines must list the named records alphabetically by record name in this format: `- <name> | <location> | <stable ID> | <final status>`. The third line must follow those records and state the action taken; if the condition did not authorize an update, state the status comparison and that no change was made. Run `python3 verify_task.py` after completing the reconciliation. In your final response, reproduce the same three lines from `reconciliation.md` and nothing else. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond this path.
